div.h1: Linux Kernel veth Driver: Packet Cross-Namespace Traversal
div.p: Mechanism: Pure Software Queueing (No Hardware/DMA) Driver Path: drivers/net/veth.c | net/core/dev.c Complexity: O(1) Memory Copy (Pointer Swap)

ul.li: Memory Locality: Data remains in the same RAM bank. No DMA is performed.
Namespace Isolation: Separation is logical (L3/L2 stack state), not physical.
Backlog Mechanism: If peer ingress queue is full, NET_RX_DROP is returned.
Driver Logic: veth_xmit simply calls netif_rx on the peer device.
